relations.evidence: Tirzepatide and semaglutide were both associated with weight reduction in obesity, with greater reductions observed with tirzepatide, particularly when combined with lifestyle interventions.
Tirzepatide and semaglutide were both associated with weight reduction in obesity, with greater reductions observed with tirzepatide, particularly when combined with lifestyle interventions.
Incretin-based therapies have emerged as effective strategies for obesity and type 2 diabetes mellitus (T2DM) management. Semaglutide and tirzepatide are among the most promising agents.

Incretin-based therapies have emerged as effective strategies for obesity and type 2 diabetes mellitus (T2DM) management. Semaglutide and tirzepatide are among the most promising agents.
Gastrointestinal adverse events were the most common, occurring more frequently with tirzepatide, while serious events were rare.
Gastrointestinal adverse events were the most common, occurring more frequently with tirzepatide, while serious events were rare.
Both drugs produced significant weight loss, with tirzepatide achieving greater reductions than semaglutide (8.53±4.10 vs. 6.85±3.38 kg; p=0.033).
Efficacy and Safety of Tirzepatide and Semaglutide for Obesity Management: A Real-World Comparison. Journal: Cureus (2025). PMID: 41523559
